data.hyperedge_name: <hyperedge>"What happens if I become totally disabled?"
<hyperedge>"If your policy ended because it lapsed, you may apply to have it put back into effect if the insured person is alive. This process is called reinstatement."

<hyperedge>"Mail the 'Request for Change of Beneficiary' to TD Life in the enclosed self-addressed return envelope. TD Life will validate and record your Change of Beneficiary and return a confirmation letter to you for your records."
<hyperedge>"TD Life Insurance Company, P .O. Box 1, TD Centre; Toronto, Ontario, M5K 1A2. TD Insurance at 1-888-788-0839."

<hyperedge>"Irrevocable Beneficiary Designation: If you name someone as an irrevocable beneficiary, you give up the right to change the beneficiary designation, unless the irrevocable beneficiary consents. This will also affect any other desired changes you may want to make to the policy in the future. In Quebec your spouse is automatically deemed to be an irrevocable beneficiary unless specifically designated as revocable."
<hyperedge>"3. Signature & Title of Owner (if a Corporation)"
<hyperedge>"If you wish to name or change your beneficiary, you must submit a written request to us, but such change shall not be binding on us until received at our Head Office."
<hyperedge>"Please review the following documents for accuracy and retain for your records: - Your Health Question Confirmation responses - Privacy Agreement"
<hyperedge>"Home and auto insurance help provide the resources you'll need should a fire, accident or break-in happen in your home or car."
<hyperedge>"Effective Date | September 7, 2023 |"

<hyperedge>"TD 20-Year Term Life insurance is one of the simplest ways to protect your family: ● Your premiums are fixed for each 20-year term and are guaranteed not to increase during that 20-year term. Premiums will increase at each renewal of a 20-year term ● Coverage renews automatically every 20 years until the insured person turns 80 years old, as long as your premiums are paid (unless you choose not to continue coverage)."

<hyperedge>"At any time, we may ask for proof that the insured person continues to be totally disabled and, if such proof is not provided, the insured person will be deemed to no longer be totally disabled on the day prior to the date on which we asked for the proof and monthly premiums will begin again."

<hyperedge>"You may apply to us within 2 years of the lapse date to have your policy reinstated. In order to reinstate your policy in that time period, you must meet all of the following criteria: - The insured person must be alive; - You must pay us all unpaid premiums due from the lapse date up to and including the reinstatement effective date."

<hyperedge>"Life insurance is the cornerstone of a sound financial plan. It can help to provide financial security for your family in the event of your death. But what if you should survive a critical illness or critical accident? With today's medical breakthroughs, more and more Canadians are surviving critical illnesses (cancer, heart attack or stroke) and injuries from critical accidents. You would need financial help to protect your lifestyle while you're recovering or retrofit your home, if necessary. That's why TD Insurance offers the Critical Illness Recovery Plan, Critical Accident Recovery Plan and an array of insurance solutions to meet your needs."
<hyperedge>"Note: TD Life cannot warrant the legal effectiveness of any change of beneficiary."
